answer.text: Simultaneously buying and selling futures of the same commodity but different expiry months
Buying and selling different but related commodities
Buying spot and selling futures of the same commodity
Buying call and selling put on the same commodity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** A calendar spread involves long and short positions in different expiry months of the SAME commodity. Lower margin is required due to reduced settlement risk. **The Trap:** Different commodities is INTER-commodity spread. Spot-futures is arbitrage. Call-put is an options strategy. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers calendar spreads in equity derivatives. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Calendar spreads have lower margin (up to 75% benefit per SEBI March 2023 circular) — they're popular for expressing views on term structure without taking directional risk.
